# Chapter 13: Living engineering: regenerative medicine, the anatomical compiler  (~90 pp)
- 13.1 Regenerative medicine: state of the art (12) · 13.2 Bioelectric morphogenesis (Levin's programme) (15) · 13.3 The anatomical compiler concept (15) · 13.4 Engineering challenges in morphogenetic control (15) · 13.5 Long-horizon framing: what it would mean to compile anatomy (15) · 13.6 Failure section: morphogenetic interventions that did not work, and what they teach (10) · 13.7 Closing: living matter as the next engineering frontier (8)

**Archetype**: control, scaling, ethics. **Project**: read the position paper for piatra-policies/cerb (the institute's CERB project) and write a one-page critique from an engineering perspective. **Exercises**: 15.
